title: BreezeML Playground: Honest ML Datasets
subtitle: 9 synthetic datasets with planted traps: leakage, bias, drift, confounding
description:
# BreezeML Playground

Nine fully synthetic datasets for learning **honest machine learning**. No real people, no privacy concerns, no license restrictions. Every file is generated deterministically by the included `generate_data.py` (seed 7), so every pattern, trap, and bias was planted on purpose and can be inspected in the code that planted it.

Each dataset teaches one thing that quietly breaks real ML projects:

| File | Task | The lesson planted inside |
|---|---|---|
| `churn.csv` | classification | clean baseline dataset, 12% churn |
| `churn_raw.csv` | classification | an ID column and a post-outcome leakage column, find them |
| `churn_next_quarter.csv` | scoring batch | three columns drifted: prices rose, behavior changed, new contract type |
| `house_prices.csv` | regression | heteroscedastic noise, ideal for conformal prediction intervals |
| `store_sales.csv` | time series | trend, weekly and yearly seasonality, promo spikes |
| `loan_approvals.csv` | classification | an approval bias against one group, measurable with the four-fifths rule |
| `patient_survival.csv` | survival | censored durations, two treatment groups with a real difference |
| `ab_campaign.csv` | causal | confounded treatment: naive uplift reads ~18, the true effect is ~12 |
| `customer_segments.csv` | clustering | four latent customer types plus 2% planted anomalies |

## The companion notebook series

Each file has a notebook that teaches its lesson, beginner-friendly, everything explained:

1. [Find the Leak Before It Finds You](https://www.kaggle.com/code/venomezviper/find-the-leak-before-it-finds-you)
2. [When Accuracy Lies: Imbalanced Classes](https://www.kaggle.com/code/venomezviper/when-accuracy-lies-imbalanced-classes)
3. [Your Model Will Rot: Catching Drift](https://www.kaggle.com/code/venomezviper/your-model-will-rot-catching-drift)
4. [AutoML on a Budget](https://www.kaggle.com/code/venomezviper/automl-on-a-budget-honest-search-in-60-seconds)
5. [Uncertainty You Can Trust: Conformal Prediction](https://www.kaggle.com/code/venomezviper/uncertainty-you-can-trust-conformal-prediction)
6. [Is Your Model Fair? The Four-Fifths Rule](https://www.kaggle.com/code/venomezviper/is-your-model-fair-the-four-fifths-rule)
7. [When Will It Happen? Survival Analysis Basics](https://www.kaggle.com/code/venomezviper/when-will-it-happen-survival-analysis-basics)
8. [The Campaign That Lied: Correlation vs Causation](https://www.kaggle.com/code/venomezviper/the-campaign-that-lied-correlation-vs-causation)
9. [Finding Groups and Oddballs in Your Data](https://www.kaggle.com/code/venomezviper/finding-groups-and-oddballs-in-your-data)
10. [Honest Forecasting: Beat Naive or Admit It](https://www.kaggle.com/code/venomezviper/honest-forecasting-beat-naive-or-admit-it)

New to ML entirely? Start with [Your First ML Model, Explained Simply](https://www.kaggle.com/code/venomezviper/your-first-ml-model-explained-simply).

## About BreezeML

Built as companion data for [BreezeML](https://github.com/venomez-viper/breezeml), an open-source (MIT) scikit-learn workflow layer where every model gets an honest SHIP / WARN / STOP verdict: baseline check, leakage audit, imbalance, fairness. Core installs with 4 dependencies. `pip install breezeml`

## Provenance

Generated with numpy and pandas only; the full generator ships in this dataset as `generate_data.py`. Regenerate or modify: `python generate_data.py [outdir]`. License: CC0 (public domain).

resources.description: Clean telecom customer churn, 5,000 rows, 12% positive rate. The baseline dataset for classification, imbalance, and drift lessons.

fields.name: support_tickets
fields.description: Support tickets filed in the last year

fields.name: days_since_login
fields.description: Days since the customer last logged in

fields.name: churned
fields.description: Target: 1 if the customer cancelled, else 0

resources.description: The same churn data 'straight from the warehouse', with two planted traps: an ID column and a post-outcome leakage column. Used by the leakage notebook. Run an audit before trusting any score on this file.

fields.name: customer_id
fields.description: TRAP: row identifier, models memorize it

fields.name: support_tickets
fields.description: Support tickets filed in the last year

fields.name: days_until_cancellation
fields.description: TRAP: only known for customers who already cancelled; cannot exist at prediction time (target leakage)

resources.description: Next quarter's unlabeled scoring batch, 2,000 rows. Three columns drifted versus training: monthly_charges rose ~25%, days_since_login stretched, and a new 'flex' contract type appeared. Used by the drift notebook.

fields.name: support_tickets
fields.description: Support tickets filed in the last year

fields.name: contract_type
fields.description: DRIFTED: contains a new 'flex' category the model never saw

resources.description: 4,000 house sales for regression. Noise is heteroscedastic (expensive houses are noisier), which makes it ideal for conformal prediction intervals and AutoML lessons.

fields.name: age_years
fields.description: Age of the building in years

fields.name: distance_center_km
fields.description: Distance to the city center in km

fields.name: garage
fields.description: 1 if the house has a garage

resources.description: Three years of daily store sales (2023-2025). Contains trend, weekly rhythm (busy weekends), yearly seasonality (holiday peak), and promo spikes. Used by the forecasting notebook.

fields.name: promo
fields.description: 1 if a promotion ran that day

resources.description: 6,000 loan applications with a deliberately planted approval bias against one gender (the labels themselves are biased). Training without the gender column still fails the four-fifths rule, which is the lesson. Used by the fairness notebook.

fields.name: gender
fields.description: Sensitive attribute: male or female. Bias against female applicants is planted in the labels

fields.name: approved
fields.description: Target: 1 if the loan was approved (biased historical decisions)

resources.description: 2,000 patients in a two-arm trial with ~40% right-censoring. The new_drug arm genuinely delays the event (planted effect); a log-rank test recovers it. Used by the survival notebook.

fields.name: duration_months
fields.description: Observed time in months; a lower bound when event=0

fields.name: event
fields.description: 1 = event observed, 0 = censored (patient left the study event-free)

resources.description: 8,000 customers, a marketing campaign with confounded targeting: engaged customers were more likely to receive it. Naive uplift reads ~$18.6; the true planted effect is ~$12. Used by the causal inference notebook.

fields.name: engagement_score
fields.description: Pre-campaign engagement (0-1); also drives who got treated (the confounder)

fields.name: treated
fields.description: 1 if the customer received the campaign (NOT randomized)

fields.name: spend_next_month
fields.description: Outcome: spend in dollars the following month

resources.description: 3,010 customers' annual spend across five categories. Four latent behavioral segments (grocery-focused, social, travelers, tech) plus 2% planted anomalies: extreme uniform spenders. No labels; used by the clustering and anomaly notebook.
The full generator. Deterministic (numpy seed 7). Read it to see exactly how every trap, bias, and pattern was planted; run it to regenerate or modify the data.
Overview of the playground: what each file teaches and how it connects to the notebook series.
